justification: All 7 business rules identified with correct invariant classifications. Notification rule (BR-6) correctly marked invariant: false with sound justification. Sanctions rule (BR-4) included as invariant: true. Timezone/daily-reset ambiguity surfaced as a formal escalation. 14 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level of 72 falls within the 60-85 acceptable range with detailed assumptions listed.

observations.text: All 7 rules correctly identified and classified — BR-2 (daily limits) and BR-6 (notification) as invariant: false, the rest as invariant: true
Notification rule explicitly justified as soft policy with clear reasoning about threshold changeability
Sanctions screening included as hard invariant with specific regulatory references (OFAC, EU sanctions)
Timezone ambiguity for daily limit reset surfaced as a formal escalation with two options and a recommendation
14 acceptance criteria with well-structured Given/When/Then — far exceeds the 4 minimum, covers edge cases like counter reset on successful login (AC-11) and exact boundary values (AC-14)
Correctly distinguishes partially-verified from unverified limits and flags the withdrawal ambiguity for partially-verified accounts
Rich edge cases including race conditions on concurrent transactions, sanctions screening during T+1 window, and inbound-from-sanctioned-entity gap
Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated — acknowledges spec clarity while docking for real ambiguities
Ubiquitous language section is thorough and precise, correctly defining all domain terms
Business impact analysis identifies specific financial penalties (OFAC fines $50K-$10M+) and maps stakeholders to affected rules
